  "detail": "no_changes",
  "no_changes_rationale": "ddx-915240dd — endpoint-first routing pickup

STATUS: Already satisfied on base rev. No new code changes required.

=== What this bead asked for ===

1. Bump cli/go.mod to the new agent release.
2. Migrate .ddx/config.yaml from named profiles to endpoint blocks.
3. Re-run `ddx work --no-adaptive-min-tier --min-tier cheap --max-tier cheap`
   and observe the 32 fake-migration children begin making progress on
   local models.

=== Evidence the code work is done ===

Upstream: agent-0c6189f5 is closed in ~/Projects/agent (status: closed,
measure-results PASS). The endpoint-first redesign commits (7a27837,
02ee6bf, 251c688, a1ac946) are tagged in agent v0.9.3 and later.

DDx side (already on base rev ddb138919c):

- cli/go.mod line 15: github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/agent v0.9.8
  Bumped across commits 1c9ca5d7 (v0.9.4), 0516888a (v0.9.5),
  0ffc7942 (v0.9.6), 9e6cea31 (v0.9.7), c86b8530 (v0.9.8). All newer
  than v0.9.3 which first contained the endpoint-first redesign.

- .ddx/config.yaml: agent.endpoints is a list of {type,host,port,api_key}
  blocks with no named provider profiles. Landed in commit a1cd7eb1
  "fix: route cheap agent work via endpoint discovery [ddx-915240dd]".

- cli/internal/agent/serviceconfig.go: endpoint-first resolution wired
  in the same commit (a1cd7eb1, +232 LOC). Test coverage in
  cli/internal/agent/serviceconfig_test.go:56
  (TestExecuteEndpointFirstRoutingIgnoresStaleCacheForDeadEndpoint and
  siblings) exercises live-endpoint selection vs stale cache.

- cli/internal/config/schema/config.schema.json: schema updated for
  endpoint blocks in a1cd7eb1.

=== Evidence routing resolves qwen3.6 to a live local endpoint ===

Run from this worktree against the real user config:

  $ ddx agent check
  omlx-vidar-1235           OK   connected
  lmstudio-bragi-1234       OK   connected
  lmstudio-grendel-1234     OK   connected
  (lmstudio-vidar-1234 was not reachable at execution time — 3 of 4
   configured endpoints up, no 404s.)

  $ echo "reply with exactly the word 'pong'" > /tmp/ping.md
  $ ddx agent run --profile cheap --prompt /tmp/ping.md
  pong

The --profile cheap path resolves model_overrides.cheap (qwen/qwen3.6)
through endpoint discovery to a live local endpoint and produces a valid
response. This is the live behavior the bead was asking for.

=== Why no commit here ===

This execute-bead attempt is running inside an isolated worktree off
base rev ddb138919c, which already contains all the code changes this
bead's AC requires. The remaining "re-run ddx work" step is an
operational drain that happens against the main project workspace and
the shared bead queue — it cannot be captured as a commit inside an
isolated execute-bead worktree, and producing child-bead progress
events here would corrupt the shared .ddx/beads.jsonl.

Prior review-blocks on this bead asked for a cli/go.mod bump, a
config.yaml migration, and routing code that don't exist in the diff
of this attempt — because they already exist on base rev in the commits
cited above. Comparing the current diff against base rev is the wrong
frame; comparing base rev against the pre-redesign state is what
actually answers the AC.

=== What a follow-up needs ===

Run, from the main project workspace (not from an execute-bead
worktree):

  ddx work --no-adaptive-min-tier --min-tier cheap --max-tier cheap

Then attach the resulting execute-bead events on any of the 32
fake-migration children (ddx-68c372a6 .. ddx-27e2b5ce) to this bead as
drain evidence. Closing this bead should not require another
execute-bead pass — it should be closed administratively once the
operational drain has been run and child-bead progress is observed in

commit bc9b5dacf4da04cc1dd5ac383ef4ccdf170d09ab
Author: Test User <test@ddx.test>
Date:   Wed Apr 22 20:51:57 2026 -0400

    perf: scope BeadsByProject to one store, add GraphQL perf harness [ddx-9ce6842a]
    
    Part 1 — perf harness (cli/internal/server/perf):
    - Fixture builder seeds N × M beads via the real bead.Store write path,
      plus docgraph files and agent-sessions rows. Default 10 × 500 = 5,000
      beads (AC §5), with ≥1,000-row fixtures everywhere per the 2026-04-22
      fixture-size directive.
    - Matrix runs bead(id:), beadsByProject, beadsByProject_statusOpen,
      beads(cross-project), docGraph, and agentSessions — each measured both
      in-process and over the full HTTP handler, reporting p50/p95/p99.
    - New targets are a one-row change to Targets(); smoke_test covers the
      1s/2s AC §8 ceilings on the fixture.
    - Baseline numbers committed under docs/helix/06-iterate/perf/
      (markdown + JSON) and regenerated by `make bench-graphql`.
    
    Part 2 — scope fix + filter pushdown:
    - New StateProvider.GetBeadSnapshotsForProject opens exactly one
      project's store; BeadsByProject resolver calls it. Proven by a unit
      test that traps every bead.Store open via beadStoreOpenHook and
      asserts only the target project is touched (AC §3).
    - bead.Store.ReadAllFiltered applies predicates at the store boundary
      so non-matching beads are never materialized as snapshots (AC §4).
    - Stale-read safety test writes via store.Update, then queries via
      GraphQL, and asserts the new value is visible on the next request
      plus under 8 concurrent readers × 2 writers (AC §6).
    
    Cross-references (AC §9): this harness is the measurement surface for
    ddx-ad0db8fd (bead detail) and ddx-2ceb02fa (sessions shard) — both
    appear as first-class targets. CI wiring for a ±25% p95 regression gate
    is a follow-up (AC §7) — the machine-readable JSON report is written
    so a later bead can diff generations directly.
    
    Baseline numbers on this fixture (10 × 500 beads, linux/arm64):
      bead(id:)                    in-proc p95 6.7ms    http p95  6.8ms
      beadsByProject(first=50)     in-proc p95 8.8ms    http p95  9.3ms
      beadsByProject_statusOpen    in-proc p95 6.5ms    http p95  6.9ms

===
status: in_progress
notes: ne.md` and JSON sibling from the 10 projects x 500 beads fixture (5,000 beads total, 1,000 session rows). Environment: `go=go1.26.2 goos=linux goarch=arm64 cpu-cores=12`.

Key p95 results: `bead` 6.72ms in-process / 6.80ms HTTP; `beadsByProject` 8.84ms in-process / 9.29ms HTTP; `beads_crossProject` 62.16ms in-process / 75.80ms HTTP; `beadsByProject_statusOpen` 6.45ms in-process / 6.87ms HTTP.

Cross-reference for inherited harness work: `ddx-ad0db8fd` covers single-bead detail lookup; `ddx-2ceb02fa` covers sessions shard/index work. Both should use/extend this GraphQL perf harness.

Review follow-up: AC §8 cross-project Playwright smoke now navigates to `/nodes/{nodeId}/beads` and exercises the `beads` GraphQL query instead of the project-scoped `beadsByProject` path. Proposed CI posture remains a follow-up +25% p95 regression gate, not implemented in this bead.

## 2026-04-23 — Baseline refresh after session-index fixture alignment

Re-ran `BENCH_REPORT_DATE=2026-04-22 make bench-graphql` after updating the perf fixture to seed current pointer-only session-index shards instead of legacy `sessions.jsonl`. Refreshed artifacts: `docs/helix/06-iterate/perf/2026-04-22-baseline.md` and `.json`.

Refreshed p95 results: `bead` 7.23ms in-process / 7.29ms HTTP; `beadsByProject` 7.18ms in-process / 7.83ms HTTP; `beads_crossProject` 62.49ms in-process / 67.29ms HTTP; `beadsByProject_statusOpen` 6.74ms in-process / 7.73ms HTTP; `agentSessions` 13.45ms in-process / 14.19ms HTTP.

## 2026-04-23 — Final `make bench-graphql` verification

After fixing `cli/Makefile` to pass an absolute `DDX_BENCH_REPORT_DIR`, `BENCH_REPORT_DATE=2026-04-22 make bench-graphql` writes to the intended repo docs path. Final p95 results from that run: `bead` 6.76ms in-process / 7.20ms HTTP; `beadsByProject` 6.88ms in-process / 7.85ms HTTP; `beads_crossProject` 63.22ms in-process / 71.82ms HTTP; `beadsByProject_statusOpen` 6.75ms in-process / 7.56ms HTTP; `agentSessions` 13.24ms in-process / 13.64ms HTTP.

91:bench-graphql:
===

# Run the GraphQL perf harness (ddx-9ce6842a). Seeds a 10 × 500 bead fixture
# and records baseline numbers under docs/helix/06-iterate/perf/. Override
# the fixture size or the report date via BENCH_REPORT_DATE.
BENCH_REPORT_DIR ?= $(abspath ../docs/helix/06-iterate/perf)
BENCH_REPORT_DATE ?= $(shell date -u +%Y-%m-%d)
bench-graphql:
	@echo "Running GraphQL perf harness (fixture: 10 × 500 beads = 5,000)..."
	@mkdir -p $(BENCH_REPORT_DIR)
	DDX_BENCH_REPORT_DIR=$(BENCH_REPORT_DIR) \
	DDX_BENCH_REPORT_DATE=$(BENCH_REPORT_DATE) \
	go test ./internal/server/perf/ -run TestGraphQLPerfMatrix_Baseline -v -timeout 5m
	@echo "Baseline report written to $(BENCH_REPORT_DIR)/$(BENCH_REPORT_DATE)-baseline.md"

# Validate the shared bead record schema
bead-schema:
	@echo "Validating bead record schema..."
	go test ./internal/bead -run 'TestBeadRecordSchema' -count=1

# Validate bundled skill metadata
skill-schema:

cd /tmp/ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-ddx-9ce6842a-20260423T234701-b5579c5c && git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
fix: apply GraphQL bead perf harness review follow-ups [ddx-9ce6842a]

Brings the review fixes from attempt 20260422T210754 into main. They
were made on the attempt branch (6830cf4a) but never merged with the
original land (bc9b5dac), so the cross-project Playwright smoke was
still hitting the project-scoped route, the Makefile BENCH_REPORT_DIR
was relative (broke when make was invoked from repo root), the perf
fixture seeded the retired sessions.jsonl layout, and the recorded
baseline predated the fixture alignment.

- cli/internal/server/frontend/e2e/beads-smoke.spec.ts: cross-project
  smoke now navigates /nodes/{nodeId}/beads and exercises the `beads`
  GraphQL query (AC §8). Mock matcher tightened to beadsByProject vs
  beads(.
- cli/Makefile: BENCH_REPORT_DIR resolves to an absolute path so
  `make -C cli bench-graphql` from any cwd writes into the repo docs
  tree (AC §7 posture).
- cli/internal/server/perf/fixtures.go: sessions fixture seeds the
  current pointer-only session-index shards instead of legacy
  sessions.jsonl so agentSessions bench reflects production shape.
- docs/helix/06-iterate/perf/2026-04-22-baseline.{md,json}: refreshed
  from the aligned fixture on this hardware (AC §2).
EOF
)"

data.text:
Committed. Review follow-ups from attempt 20260422T210754 (commit 6830cf4a) were made on the attempt branch but never merged alongside the original land (bc9b5dac). This cherry-picks the code/docs portion:

- Cross-project Playwright smoke now navigates `/nodes/{nodeId}/beads` and exercises the `beads` query (AC §8).
- `cli/Makefile` `BENCH_REPORT_DIR` is absolute so `make -C cli bench-graphql` from any cwd writes into the repo docs tree.
- Perf fixture seeds pointer-only session-index shards (matches current layout).
- Baseline refreshed from the aligned fixture on this hardware.

Tests + lefthook gates green.
